description: Some truths follow from following truths.

hints.text: Three [premise|premises] this time: $A$, $A \to B$, and $B \to C$. The goal is $C$. You'll need to apply [modus-ponens|modus ponens] twice — once to reach the middle stop, then again to reach the destination. Start by assuming all three premises.
Now assume $A \to B$ and $B \to C$ as well. You'll need them both.
Apply [modus-ponens|modus ponens] with $A \to B$ (step 2) and $A$ (step 1) to reach $B$.
Now apply [modus-ponens|modus ponens] again with $B \to C$ (step 3) and your freshly-derived $B$ to reach $C$.
